text.title: HN Launch Examples: Success and Failure
text.summary: Successful developer tool Show HN (what worked, results); failed Show HN with marketing language (why it failed, consequences).

example.note: Post: "Show HN: I built a terminal file manager in Rust". All success factors present.
example.code:
Success factors: - Descriptive, non-hypey title (Rust = technically credible) - Open source with GitHub link (community can inspect and contribute) - Fast, polished demo without signup - Author responded to every comment within minutes - Honestly acknowledged what the tool doesn't do - Fixed two bugs reported in comments within the day
Results: Points: 500+ Time on front page: 12 hours GitHub stars gained: 2,000 Contributors who joined: 50 Traffic: ~80,000 visits in 48 hours

example.note: Post: "Show HN: Revolutionary AI-powered productivity app". All failure patterns present.
example.code:
Failure factors: - Marketing language in title ("revolutionary") - Vague description (what does it actually do?) - Required signup before seeing any functionality - Defensive replies to critical comments - No technical details shared
Results: Points: 3 Flagged as self-promotion within 20 minutes Negative comment thread remained as search result Post disappeared from /newest within 1 hour
